title: For Trump, a Promised Economic Boom Collides With the Costs of War
summary: President Trump had envisioned a growing economy and improving fortunes for American families in 2026. That appears at risk in his war with Iran.

title: How Migrant Workers Have Been Affected by Iran’s Strikes
summary: Since the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran began, Iran has fired hundreds of missiles and drones in retaliation at Gulf countries. Iranian officials say that they are attacking U.S. military bases and American interests in the Gulf countries, not civilian targets. Our reporter Vivian Nereim talks with Katrin Bennhold about how migrant workers have been affected.

title: Pentagon Names 6 Military Members Killed in Iraq Tanker Crash
summary: The crew members had been part of the U.S. and Israel’s war on Iran, bringing the death toll of American service members in the conflict to at least 13.

title: Judge Rules Lawmaker Must Be Allowed to Join Kennedy Center Board Meeting
summary: A judge ordered that Representative Joyce Beatty, a Democrat, be given access to documents and have the chance to oppose changes to the center at next week’s board meeting.

title: In Scolding the New U.S. Ambassador, South Africa Signals Defiance
summary: The latest clash between the two countries illustrates the depths to which relations have sunk and the struggle to forge a new path forward.

title: Trump Pardons: Lobbyist Charged in Extortion Attempt
summary: Josh Nass had recently represented Joseph Schwartz, a former nursing home executive who was convicted of tax crimes before being freed by the president late last year.

title: How Trump Officials Embraced an Animal Rights Campaign
summary: The Trump administration is curbing animal experiments in response to shifts in public opinion, technological advances, years of animal rights advocacy and the work of a conservative activist.

title: Synagogue Attack Tests Bonds of Michigan Community
summary: For more than a century, Jewish, Arab and Muslim people have lived in and around Detroit, coexisting mostly peacefully.

title: Explosion at Jewish School in Amsterdam Is 2nd Antisemitic Attack in 2 Days, Officials Say
summary: No one was hurt in the explosion at a Jewish school in Amsterdam, or in an earlier one at a Rotterdam synagogue.

title: How Jeff Bezos Upended The Washington Post
summary: The billionaire newspaper owner, dissatisfied by years of losses, wants the newsroom to double productivity with half its budget.

title: The Man Behind the Oscars ‘Glambot’ on the Red Carpet
summary: Cole Walliser grabs celebrities for red-carpet close-ups. In recent years, he has become known in his own right, for better or for worse.

title: Prediction Markets? An 83% Chance That Oscars Pundits Hate Them.
summary: Online wagering is all the rage. But the crowdsourced data generally doesn’t interest experts who have built a brand predicting Academy Awards races.

title: What to Watch for at the 2026 Oscars: Best Actor Race, Conan O’Brien and More
summary: It will be a tight race for best picture and actor, while the In Memoriam segment looks to be supersized this year.

title: Trump Wins When Oscar Winners Stay Silent
summary: Right now it does matter a lot whether actors can find the right words.

title: What Trump Didn’t Know About Iran
summary: Ali Vaez, the Iran project director at the International Crisis Group, explains the history of miscalculations that led to the war in Iran.

title: Some Olympic Leaders Want to See Fixed Winter Games Host Cities
summary: Fewer cities are bidding for Olympics, and those that are can’t always accommodate every event. The Milan-Cortina Games were the most sprawling Olympics in history.

title: Marseille’s Tight Mayoral Race Is a Bellwether for France’s Future
summary: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.

title: ‘How Low Can You Go?’ The Shifting Guidelines for Blood Pressure Control
summary: The number doctors use to demarcate hypertension keeps going down, a trend applauded by many experts, who point to studies linking high blood pressure and dementia.

title: Lily Collins’s Ring, Stolen Years Ago, Makes an Unexpected Return
summary: The ring, which belonged to Ms. Collins, the “Emily in Paris” actress, was stolen in 2023. Joe Hakimian, a Chicago jeweler, unknowingly listed it for sale in December. Then came a surprising message.
